title: Pilosella flagellariformis
intro: Pilosella flagellariformis, also known as Hawkweed, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the Asteraceae family, characterized by its yellow flowers and milky sap. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, offering potential health benefits in treating wounds, reducing pain and inflammation, and promoting wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Pilosella flagellariformis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpene lactones, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Pilosella flagellariformis is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and topical creams, which are traditionally used to treat skin conditions and promote tissue repair.
taxonomy: Pilosella flagellariformis is a species of plant in the family Rosaceae. It is a member of the tribe Cichorieae, which is a group of dandelion-like plants. This plant is often classified as a type of hawk's beard, with some sources considering it a synonym of Pilosella flagellaris. However, further research may be needed to confirm its classification.
